title: What Ancient History Actually Is
body:
Ancient history examines the societies that came before the modern world. It is built from inscriptions, ruins, objects, texts, and the careful interpretation of evidence that survives by accident as much as design.

The field includes Mesopotamia, Egypt, Greece, Rome, early China, India, the ancient Americas, and the many smaller societies that interacted with them.

Ancient history is also a reminder that civilisation is not new. Humans have been organising, fighting, trading, and imagining meaning for thousands of years.

The field asks how those societies worked, how they changed, and why some endured while others collapsed or transformed.
keyPoints: Ancient history depends on fragmentary evidence
Archaeology and texts must be read together
Religion, law, and power were deeply linked
Collapse and transition were recurring patterns
furtherReading: The Ancient City by Joseph Rykwert
The Dawn of Everything by David Graeber and David Wengrow
